extraction.one_line_summary: MOLARD Rudy attacked solo under 2km to go on the uphill finish to Vence, resisting late-chase favorites after POELS Wout abandoned.
tactical_patterns: MOLARD attacked inside final 2km on uphill finish, demonstrating that late solo moves work when peloton hesitates after regrouping.
DE GENDT Thomas positioned in intermediate break but was neutralized on flat section before final climb, showing break timing limits.
Favorites waited until 5km to react after catching escapees at 25km, revealing hesitation creates windows for outsider attacks.

rider_signals.MOLARD Rudy: positive: First World Tour win with perfectly timed late attack on uphill finish, lives locally and pre-rode course extensively.
rider_signals.POELS Wout: negative: Abandoned while second overall during climb of Col de l'Ecre, significant weakness or mechanical issue ended GC challenge.
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Strongest among outsiders on penultimate climb, first over summit but couldn't sustain advantage with WELLENS Tim.
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: Bridged to YATES on final climb and finished second, showing stronger finishing kick than initial attacker.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Finished third but couldn't respond to MOLARD's late move, suggesti ng punch riders struggle with very late accelerations.
rider_signals.SÁNCHEZ Luis León: positive: Retained yellow jersey finishing with favorites group, successfully defended 22-second margin over ALAPHILIPPE Julian.
